title: Ransomware Pre-Encryption
briefing: On $TARGET, shadow copies were deleted (vssadmin delete shadows /all) and then a process began rapidly creating files with a new extension across a user's Documents folder. Destroying backups then mass-rewriting files is the immediate pre-encryption / encryption phase of ransomware. This is a live emergency — recognise it fast and scope it.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 100144 (vssadmin shadow delete) then rule.id: 100161 (rapid file creation). Correlate on the same computer = $TARGET.

questions.explain: Shadow-copy deletion followed by mass file creation with a new extension = active ransomware.

questions.prompt: Which two behaviours together confirm ransomware here? (select all)
options: Rapid mass file creation with a new extension
answer: Rapid mass file creation with a new extension
questions.explain: Inhibiting recovery (shadow delete) + high-rate file rewrite is the ransomware signature.
Both the vssadmin and file-create events carry computer = $TARGET.
T1486 (encryption) + T1490 (shadow-copy deletion to block restore).

options: Identify and kill the encrypting process
Check whether it is spreading to other hosts / shares
Preserve a memory image before wipe if possible
Wait for the encryption to finish
answer: Identify and kill the encrypting process
Check whether it is spreading to other hosts / shares
Preserve a memory image before wipe if possible
questions.explain: Speed matters: isolate, kill the process, check for spread, and grab volatile evidence — never let it finish.
